summary: EXP-238 resolves ISS-1482 (c4 H2, major, internal Kp inconsistency in WIN-025) with three targeted substring patches. Independent audit of Chernogor & Holub (2024) and the GFZ Potsdam Kp archive confirmed the paper's 'quiet conditions (Kp ≤ 3)' claim — peak Kp 3.3 across April 7–9, 2024, and Kp 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality (15–21 UTC, April 8). The dome author's withdrawal-rationale claim of 'Kp ~5–6' is not supported by the official record. Patches: (1) detail_evidence gains one acknowledgment sentence citing GFZ Potsdam as independent corroboration and forward-refs the dome-removal dispute; (2) detail_extra paragraph 1 qualifies '(Kp ~5–6)' as author-stated and points to the audit; (3) detail_extra paragraph 2 softens 'commendable scientific self-correction' to acknowledge the rationale is factually incorrect while preserving credit for the withdrawal act itself. All three before-strings verified to occur exactly once. No other WIN-025 fields touched. Parallel narrative in data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 flagged as cross-file consistency issue requiring a separate follow-up EXP.
newsworthy: Dome author removed a 'confirmed WIN' citing a geomagnetic storm that the official Kp record says never happened — April 8 eclipse-hour Kp was 0–1.3, not 5–6.
